branching.note: B1 = expected number of (tile,rotation) candidates when 1 edge is constrained. B2 = when 2 adjacent edges are constrained (weighted by pair frequency).

search_advice.justification: The largest forced cluster has 2 tiles linked by forced adjacencies (unique-pattern pairs), giving a fixed-orientation anchor. Expanding along the boundary of this cluster on a triangular surface typically constrains 2 edges per new slot when the frontier is kept convex. The weighted average branching factor drops from 12.9 (1-edge constraint) to 0.4 (2-edge constraint), making a 2-constrained-edge frontier essential. The 7 forced pairs and 5 near-forced pairs should be assigned first to fix orientation ambiguities before the main search.
